questions.text: Who are the 'People of the Heights' (Ashab al-A'raf) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: People whose good and bad deeds are equal; they stand on a wall between Paradise and Hell, hoping for Allah's mercy before eventually entering Paradise.

questions.text: What was the specific argument Iblis used to justify his refusal to bow to Adam?
questions.answer: Racist superiority: 'I am better than him. You created me from fire and created him from clay'.

questions.text: What 'clothing' does Allah describe as the best in Verse 26?
What happened to the people of Prophet Nuh when they rejected his message?
questions.answer: They were drowned in the Great Flood.

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people of 'Ad, and what was their punishment?
questions.answer: Prophet Hud; they were destroyed by a barren, screaming wind.

questions.text: What was the miraculous sign given to the people of Thamud (Prophet Salih)?
questions.answer: A She-Camel that emerged from a rock as a clear sign from Allah.

questions.text: In the story of Musa (AS), what happened when Allah revealed His glory to the mountain?
questions.answer: The mountain crumbled to dust and Musa fell down unconscious.

questions.text: What was the 'Covenant of the Souls' mentioned in Verse 172?
questions.answer: Allah brought forth the descendants of Adam from their loins and made them testify: 'Am I not your Lord?' They all replied, 'Yes, we testify'.

questions.text: What is the 'Sabbath' violation mentioned regarding a town by the sea?
questions.answer: The people used tricks to catch fish on the Sabbath (Saturday) after being forbidden to do so, and were subsequently transformed into apes.

questions.text: How should a believer respond when the Quran is recited, according to Verse 204?
questions.answer: Listen to it attentively and remain silent so that they may receive mercy.
